text:
Bubble wrap. The satisfaction is immediate and complete. Each pop delivers a small, perfect resistance followed by collapse, and then it's done, that cell gone. There's no ambiguity about it. You press, it yields, it pops. The sound is clean. The tactile feedback is precise.

What makes it oddly delightful is that it serves no purpose beyond its original packaging function, yet the compulsion to pop every single bubble is nearly universal. People will work through an entire sheet methodically. It doesn't matter who you are. The urge is the same. That kind of cross-cultural, cross-demographic agreement on something so trivial feels almost like discovering a fact about human nature.
